deep_dive.title: Beyond Detection: Agentic Attack Synthesis and Simulation for Smart Contracts
deep_dive.abstract: Smart contract vulnerabilities pose severe financial risks, yet existing security tools largely stop at vulnerability detection, offering limited support for explaining whether reported flaws are exploitable, how attacks unfold, and what concrete damage they cause. To bridge this gap, we propose KASS (Knowledge-Augmented Attack Synthesis and Simulation), a multi-agent framework for executable smart contract exploit verification. KASS decomposes automated exploit generation into planning, generation, and testing stages, and integrates three complementary mechanisms: retrieval-augmented planning over real-world audit knowledge, formal generation and validation constraints that bind attack plans to executable proof-of-concept tests, and a hierarchical dual-loop refinement process that repairs code-level errors while triggering strategy-level replanning when attack assumptions fail. We evaluate KASS on 104 SmartBugs-Curated contracts across four vulnerability categories. Experimental results show that KASS successfully generates executable exploits for 94.23% of tested contracts; this rate is higher than previously reported results for REX and AdvSCanner on comparable SmartBugs-Curated subsets, and higher than our reproduced Claude Code baseline under the same evaluation protocol. On 11 real-world CVE-tagged contracts, KASS successfully validates 9 cases. Beyond exploit generation, KASS produces structured attack plans that document exploitation flows, quantify potential asset losses, and serve as semantic false positive filters for static analysis tools.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Multi-agent framework for smart contract vulnerability exploitation and verification
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向智能合约漏洞利用与验证的多智能体框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向智能合约漏洞利用与验证的多智能体框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向智能合约漏洞利用与验证的多智能体框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: KASS automates exploit generation for smart contracts, combining retrieval-augmented planning, formal constraints, and hierarchical simulation to verify vulnerability exploitability.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有智能合约安全工具多止步于漏洞检测，缺乏对漏洞可利用性、攻击流程及具体损失的自动解释。KASS提出多智能体框架，将利用生成分解为规划、生成与测试阶段，集成检索增强规划、形式化约束验证及分层模拟，实现可执行的漏洞利用验证。实验证明KASS能有效生成并验证真实世界智能合约攻击，推动安全审计自动化。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: Advanced techniques for software vulnerability detection and exploitation prevention

deep_dive.title: A Modular Framework for Stack-Heap and Value Abstractions (Extended Version)
deep_dive.abstract: Advanced static program analysis requires reasoning on the semantics of non-trivial program behaviors (e.g., pointers and complex data structures such as lists and sets, functions, and objects) and how they affect the memory. In most programming languages, static and dynamic allocations are typically managed by the stack and the heap, respectively. However, how allocations behave and how the memory is managed at runtime can vary significantly depending on the programming language being analyzed. Proper handling of these aspects is essential, as an accurate memory model enables the detection of critical issues such as buffer overflows and underflows, use-after-free errors, and null pointer exceptions prior to execution, that is, before such erroneous behaviors occur. In this paper, we propose and formalize a generic memory framework to handle stack and heap memory during the analysis, that is able to support various behaviors from different programming languages (e.g., C, C++, Java, and Python), while remaining parametric, allowing different memory and value analyses to be independently chosen and combined. It relies on the Abstract Interpretation theory and enables sound approximation of different memory models and program behaviors. We introduce a split state abstraction that separates value and memory analyses into two modular abstract domains. These domains interact through a set of memory identifiers, along with a set of operations defined by the domains to manipulate them, allowing the framework to capture both value information and structural memory relationships.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Static analysis framework for memory error detection (buffer overflows, use-after-free) before execution
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向内存错误检测的静态分析框架，支持执行前发现缓冲区溢出等漏洞
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向内存错误检测的静态分析框架，支持执行前发现缓冲区溢出等漏洞
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向内存错误检测的静态分析框架，支持执行前发现缓冲区溢出等漏洞
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a modular static analysis framework with accurate stack-heap memory models to detect critical bugs like buffer overflows and use-after-free in various languages, enhancing software security.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 不同编程语言的内存分配与运行时行为差异大，准确内存模型对于静态分析至关重要。本文提出模块化框架，通过可定制的栈堆抽象与值抽象，支持跨语言高级静态分析，能在执行前检测缓冲区溢出、释放后使用等关键内存错误，有效提升软件安全性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 不同编程语言的内存分配与运行时行为差异大，准确内存模型对于静态分析至关重要。本文提出模块化框架，通过可定制的栈堆抽象与值抽象，支持跨语言高级静态分析，能在执行前检测缓冲区溢出、释放后使用等关键内存错误，有效提升软件安全性。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: static analysis of binary software for security

quick_skim.title: Open-Source Intelligence for Code Provenance and the Security Patterns that Separate Human and Large-Language-Model Implementations of Common Programming Tasks
quick_skim.abstract: Developers now draw code from two very different sources, the accumulated human answers on sites such as Stack Overflow and the output of large language models. We ask two questions about that split. First, can the provenance of a code snippet be recovered from the code itself, and second, do the two sources differ in the security patterns they adopt for the same task. Using only open sources, a public gateway of open-weight language models and the public Stack Overflow API, we build a fully reproducible pipeline that collects real implementations of 31 security-sensitive programming tasks, among them OAuth with PKCE, JWT verification, password hashing, and SQL access, from 9 language models and from human answers, and scores every sample with deterministic security and style detectors. On 528 real samples we train a cross-validated classifier that recovers human versus model provenance with 93 percent accuracy against a 78 percent baseline, and a 7-way classifier that attributes a sample to the specific model that wrote it at 48 percent. We then report where the sources diverge on security, which patterns models adopt more often than the human corpus and which they inherit from it. Running the same tasks in Python, JavaScript, Go, and Java, we find the security divergence holds in every language while the provenance boundary is partly language-specific and does not transfer symmetrically between them. A vulnerability repair case study, in which models are handed insecure code and asked to fix it, finds a 77 percent repair rate across 21 seeds and 12 weakness classes, but a recurring partial-fix failure in which the model removes the insecure pattern without adding the correct defense. The pipeline is data driven, so any new task or language is added as a single specification entry, and a fail-closed checker re-derives every number in this paper from the stored data.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: analyzes security patterns in code snippets, distinguishing human vs. LLM code, a form of static analysis for security
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 分析代码片段的安全模式，区分人类与LLM代码，属于安全静态分析
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 分析代码片段的安全模式，区分人类与LLM代码，属于安全静态分析
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 分析代码片段的安全模式，区分人类与LLM代码，属于安全静态分析
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Studies provenance and security patterns of code from Stack Overflow and LLMs, finding LLM code often uses different, sometimes less secure patterns in security-sensitive tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对开发者同时从Stack Overflow和LLM获取代码的现状，本文构建可复现管道收集31项安全关键任务的真实实现，分析代码来源识别和两种来源的安全模式差异。发现LLM代码在OAuth、JWT验证等任务中采用不同的安全实践，有时更不安全，为安全静态分析提供了新视角。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对开发者同时从Stack Overflow和LLM获取代码的现状，本文构建可复现管道收集31项安全关键任务的真实实现，分析代码来源识别和两种来源的安全模式差异。发现LLM代码在OAuth、JWT验证等任务中采用不同的安全实践，有时更不安全，为安全静态分析提供了新视角。

quick_skim.title: Ciphertext- and Polynomial-Level Optimization for Fully Homomorphic Encryption
quick_skim.abstract: Fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) schemes such as RNS-CKKS enable privacy-preserving services by allowing direct computation on encrypted data. While recent FHE compilers optimize FHE programs, they operate at the coarse-grained ciphertext level, where each ciphertext operation comprises a sequence of polynomial operations. At this granularity, the compilers miss optimization opportunities across ciphertext operations. This work presents Recifhe, a new multi-level compiler that supports not only ciphertext-level but also polynomial-level optimization. At the ciphertext level, Recifhe transforms a non-FHE input program into an FHE program by inserting ciphertext management operations and applies global optimizations. At the polynomial level, Recifhe eliminates redundant polynomial computations across ciphertext operations. Recifhe achieves a 1.25x speedup over ciphertext-level-only optimization.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Optimizes fully homomorphic encryption compilers for practical confidential computing on encrypted data.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 优化全同态加密编译器以实现加密数据上的实用机密计算。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 优化全同态加密编译器以实现加密数据上的实用机密计算。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 优化全同态加密编译器以实现加密数据上的实用机密计算。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper presents Recifhe, a multi-level compiler that performs ciphertext-level and polynomial-level optimizations for FHE programs, significantly improving performance and enabling more practical privacy-preserving computation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对全同态加密(FHE)编译器仅在密文级优化而忽略跨操作优化机会的问题，本文提出多级编译器Recifhe，同时支持密文级与多项式级优化。实验表明该方法能显著提升FHE程序性能，降低计算开销。该工作为机密计算领域的实用化提供了重要的编译优化支持，有助于推动隐私保护计算的发展。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对全同态加密(FHE)编译器仅在密文级优化而忽略跨操作优化机会的问题，本文提出多级编译器Recifhe，同时支持密文级与多项式级优化。实验表明该方法能显著提升FHE程序性能，降低计算开销。该工作为机密计算领域的实用化提供了重要的编译优化支持，有助于推动隐私保护计算的发展。

quick_skim.title: SQUIRO: A Framework for Security-Aware Quantum-Classical Scheduling on Kubernetes
quick_skim.abstract: Distributed infrastructure schedulers traditionally optimise capacity, locality, and cost, but provide limited support for security posture and emerging quantum-classical workloads. As hybrid quantum-classical computing becomes increasingly practical and post-quantum security requirements begin to affect infrastructure deployment, schedulers must jointly reason about heterogeneous compute resources, security constraints, and quantum backend characteristics. We present SQUIRO, a framework for security-aware quantum-classical scheduling based on a platform-independent Unified Scheduling Model (USM) and a six-step Scheduler Design Methodology (SDM) that together enable the derivation of concrete schedulers for Kubernetes, high-performance computing (HPC), and federated environments. The framework combines multidimensional security posture enforcement through hard feasibility constraints with residual-risk optimisation, and introduces a circuit-aware quantum backend selector that accounts for coherence margin, calibration freshness, queue pressure, and hardware capabilities through a forward-compatible colocation hierarchy. Evaluation on synthetic Kubernetes clusters shows that the security model enforces complete compliance for regulated workloads by construction, while global optimisation reduces infrastructure cost by up to 51% and energy consumption by up to 63% compared with greedy placement in underloaded scenarios, without compromising admission priorities. Additional experiments characterise the solve-time growth of the current CP-SAT formulation and show that circuit-aware backend selection systematically diverges from naive error-rate ranking under coherence- and queue-limited conditions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Presents a security-aware scheduler for heterogeneous quantum-classical workloads.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出针对量子-经典异构工作负载的安全感知调度器
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出针对量子-经典异构工作负载的安全感知调度器
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出针对量子-经典异构工作负载的安全感知调度器
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SQUIRO provides a security-aware scheduling framework for hybrid quantum-classical computing on Kubernetes, integrating security constraints with resource management.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 分布式调度器通常未充分考虑安全态势和量子-经典混合负载的调度需求。SQUIRO提出一种平台无关的统一调度模型和六步设计方法学，为Kubernetes等平台导出具体调度器，联合推理异构计算资源、安全约束和量子后端特性。实验表明该框架能够有效平衡安全与性能，为异构计算环境中的安全调度提供了通用参考实现。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 分布式调度器通常未充分考虑安全态势和量子-经典混合负载的调度需求。SQUIRO提出一种平台无关的统一调度模型和六步设计方法学，为Kubernetes等平台导出具体调度器，联合推理异构计算资源、安全约束和量子后端特性。实验表明该框架能够有效平衡安全与性能，为异构计算环境中的安全调度提供了通用参考实现。
